mechanism_narrative: Persistent decline in industrial production signals a deepening recession, reducing real growth expectations and causing a flight to long-term Treasuries, which compresses yields over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward yield rises in >60% of instances when INDPRO is below its 6-month average, or rank IC <0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above a threshold of 4.5%, it triggers expectations of further labor market weakness and aggressive Fed easing, reducing long-term yields through lower real rate expectations.
failure_condition: 10-year yield rises in >50% of instances within 5 days after UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or average yield change is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and retail sales reveal a synchronized demand collapse, reinforcing recession expectations and prompting a rush into safe-haven Treasuries, driving yields sharply lower.
failure_condition: Average 20-day yield change is positive when both INDPRO and RSAFS are below their respective 3-month moving averages, or joint signal yields rank IC below 0.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary easing cycle, a declining federal funds rate signals lower short-term rates ahead, which drags down long-term yields through expectations of persistently accommodative policy.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed cut rates from 5.25% to 2% by April 2008
failure_condition: During easing periods (FEDFUNDS declining over 6 months), 60-day yield changes are positive more than 40% of the time, or the correlation between weekly FEDFUNDS changes and yield changes is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts indicates severe contraction in residential investment, a leading indicator of recession, which reduces economic growth expectations and pushes Treasury yields lower.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts fell from over 2M in 2005 to under 1M in 2008
failure_condition: 20-day yield rises when HOUST is at cycle lows, or the annualized Sharpe ratio of the HOUST-based signal is below 0.5 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 315, it signals elevated inflation persistence, forcing the market to price higher term premiums and pushing long-term yields upward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 20-day yield change is negative after CPIAUCSL exceeds 315, or rank IC less than 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with falling payroll employment amplifies labor market deterioration signals, reinforcing recession expectations and accelerating the decline in long-term yields via flight-to-safety.
failure_condition: When both UNRATE rises and PAYEMS falls over the same month, the subsequent 5-day yield change is positive in >40% of instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant in a regression.

mechanism_narrative: In the immediate aftermath of a major financial crisis (e.g., Lehman bankruptcy), extreme risk aversion triggers a massive flight to safety, compressing Treasury yields regardless of other macro signals.
evidence_refs: fact:Lehman Brothers filed for bankruptcy on 2008-09-15
failure_condition: 10-year yield rises within 5 days of the Lehman event, or the average yield change in crisis windows is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
